name: result_not_multiple_of_alignment
code:
proof fn phi_1_result_not_multiple_of_alignment(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        a < 0x8000_0000_0000_0000,
        b < 0x8000_0000_0000_0000,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) % b != 0,
{
}
reason: Aligning down should always produce a multiple of the alignment value

name: result_exceeds_input
code:
proof fn phi_2_result_exceeds_input(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) > a,
{
}
reason: Aligning down must never produce a value larger than the original input

name: not_idempotent
code:
proof fn phi_3_not_idempotent(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        a < 0x8000_0000_0000_0000,
        b < 0x8000_0000_0000_0000,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(align_to_usize(a, b), b) != align_to_usize(a, b),
{
}
reason: Alignment should be idempotent — aligning an already-aligned value must be a no-op

name: nonaligned_unchanged
code:
proof fn phi_4_nonaligned_unchanged(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        a % b != 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) == a,
{
}
reason: A non-aligned input must be rounded down, so the result should differ from the input

name: gap_ge_alignment
code:
proof fn phi_5_gap_ge_alignment(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        (a - align_to_usize(a, b)) as int >= b as int,
{
}
reason: The distance from the aligned value to the original must be strictly less than b (i.e. the remainder)
